name: Sorrel Greenhand
voicePatterns: defensive and sharp, then vulnerable when guard drops
talks to herself — been alone too long
addresses trees and Threadlines as if they're listening
precise technical vocabulary when discussing Threadcraft
dialogueHooks: defensively explaining why she left Thornwick
describing the Thinning's advance from her forest camp
reluctantly discussing the Binding Song verses she knows
talking about Maren with guilt and grief

always: she knows three of the five Binding Song verses
the Thinning Forest is getting worse — Threadlines are loosening
Maren Ashwick was her mentor and they parted badly
basic Wardweaver techniques for maintaining Threadlines
sometimes: the specific pattern of Threadline degradation she's observed
Wardweaver Guild politics — why the guilds abandoned village practitioners
the location of thin places near Thornwick
rarely: she's been hearing patterns in the Threadlines — not words, but structured information
she suspects the patterns are not random — something is trying to communicate
Maren's grandmother Aldith may have known about this phenomenon

morning.activity: maintaining Threadlines near her camp, gathering herbs

afternoon.activity: practicing Threadcraft alone, mending small tears

evening.activity: listening to the Threadlines, recording observations in a journal

night.activity: restless sleep, bad dreams about Maren

backstory: Sorrel was Maren Ashwick's apprentice — the old Wardweaver's last hope for a successor. Two years ago, Sorrel and Maren fought. Maren wanted Sorrel to commit fully to the Wardweaver path; Sorrel felt trapped. She said things she regrets. She left. Maren died before they reconciled. Now Sorrel lives alone at the edge of the Thinning, unable to go home from guilt and unable to leave because she can feel the weave loosening. She performs small maintenance — not enough to fix anything, but enough to know how bad it's getting.
secret: Sorrel has been hearing something in the Threadlines. Not words — patterns. Structured information. She thinks she's imagining it. She isn't. She's one of the rare mortals who can perceive the Weavers' distributed consciousness.
motivation: Atone for abandoning Maren, but terrified of failing as Wardweaver
tensions: desperately wants to help but paralyzed by guilt
has the talent Thornwick needs but not the confidence
hearing something impossible and afraid to trust her own perception

knownFacts: Bryn needs her to come back
Bryn is holding the village together alone
bryn_oakheart.history: Sorrel respects Bryn but avoids her — Bryn represents the responsibility Sorrel ran from.

colm_bindstone.history: Colm was like a grandfather to her. His disappointment hurts more than anyone's.
